verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 is a multi-pass TM IP3-gated Ca2+ release channel canonically resident in the ER membrane (endomembrane). However, a documented minority pool localizes to the plasma membrane in several cell types (e.g., polarized epithelia, B cells, platelets), where it can function as a PM Ca2+ channel with extracellular-facing loops accessible from outside. This dual ER/PM distribution qualifies as contextual via dual_localization. Not pMHC-only; not purely ER-resident.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Strength/breadth of documented PM pool for ITPR3 specifically vs ITPR1; if PM localization is not robustly established for this isoform, correct call would be endomembrane_resident.
